analysis_steps: Identified single dominant peak at 1369 cm⁻¹ as the primary spectral feature
Compared peak position against known Raman-active modes: hBN E₂g (~1366–1370 cm⁻¹) is the closest match
Considered graphite/carbon D-band (~1350 cm⁻¹), but absence of G-band (~1580 cm⁻¹) makes this unlikely for a pure carbon material
Evaluated FWHM of 29 cm⁻¹: consistent with well-crystallized hBN; amorphous BN would show much broader features
Assessed layer number from peak position: 1369 cm⁻¹ is typical for bulk hBN; monolayer hBN would show slight blue shift to ~1370–1374 cm⁻¹
Confirmed sample purity: no secondary peaks or baseline anomalies; single-phase crystalline hBN is the simplest explanation
Assigned AA' stacking order as the thermodynamically stable bulk hBN polytype
decision_trace: Single dominant peak at 1369 cm⁻¹ matches hBN E₂g phonon mode (literature: 1366–1370 cm⁻¹ for bulk)
No D (~1350 cm⁻¹) and G (~1580 cm⁻¹) pair rules out graphitic carbon
FWHM of 29 cm⁻¹ consistent with crystalline hBN
High prominence (0.97) and clean spectrum suggest pure single-phase material
Peak position not blue-shifted, consistent with bulk rather than mono/few-layer hBN
No additional peaks observed that would indicate contamination or mixed phases
